objects.name: Coordinated Credential-Based VPN Authentication Attacks (Dec 2023)
objects.description: A coordinated, automated campaign targeting enterprise VPN authentication infrastructure, with large-scale scripted credential-based login attempts observed against Cisco SSL VPN and Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect services. The campaign utilized centralized, cloud-hosted infrastructure (primarily 3xK GmbH IP space) and employed uniform request patterns and reused credentials, indicating automated password spraying or credential stuffing.
objects.objective: Identify and exploit exposed or weakly protected VPN authentication endpoints via automated credential-based attacks.

objects.name: Automated Credential-Based Authentication Attack
objects.description: Automated login attempts using common username and password combinations to probe VPN authentication endpoints for weak or exposed credentials. Includes scripted password spraying and credential stuffing techniques.
objects.x_mitre_detection: Monitor for high volumes of failed authentication attempts, uniform user agent strings, and centralized IP address sources.

objects.name: 3xK GmbH Cloud Hosting
objects.description: Centralized cloud-hosted infrastructure used to launch coordinated automated credential-based attacks against VPN authentication endpoints. Majority of traffic originated from IP space associated with 3xK GmbH (Germany).

objects.name: GreyNoise Intelligence
objects.description: GreyNoise Intelligence is a threat intelligence provider that tracks and analyzes internet-scale threat activity, including credential-based attacks targeting enterprise VPN infrastructure.
Common username combinations reused in scripted login attempts
Common password combinations reused in scripted login attempts

objects.name: Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect
objects.description: VPN authentication portal targeted by automated credential-based campaign

objects.name: Cisco SSL VPN
objects.description: VPN authentication endpoint targeted by automated credential-based campaign
Observed request bodies include CSRF token handling and parameterized username/password fields

objects.name: Mozilla Firefox
objects.description: User agent used in most observed requests targeting GlobalProtect portals

objects.name: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)
objects.description: Dominant user agent in Cisco SSL VPN bruteforce attempts
The coordinated credential-based campaign used automated VPN credential probing scripts to conduct large-scale login attempts.
The campaign employed automated credential-based authentication attacks, including password spraying and credential stuffing.
The campaign leveraged 3xK GmbH cloud-hosted infrastructure to launch coordinated attacks.
GreyNoise VPN Login Scanner was used to detect and analyze activity associated with the campaign.
The campaign targeted Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN authentication portals.
The campaign targeted Cisco SSL VPN authentication endpoints.
The automated VPN credential probing script implements automated credential-based authentication attacks.
The probing script was operated from centralized cloud-hosted infrastructure (3xK GmbH).
The probing script reused common username combinations in login attempts.
The probing script reused common password combinations in login attempts.
The probing script targeted Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN portals.
The probing script targeted Cisco SSL VPN endpoints.
Automated credential-based authentication attacks include request bodies with CSRF token handling and parameterized username/password fields.
Automated credential-based authentication attempts over TCP targeted Palo Alto Networks GlobalProtect VPN endpoints.
Automated credential-based authentication attempts over TCP targeted Cisco SSL VPN endpoints.
The network traffic originated from IP space associated with 3xK GmbH.
The network traffic originated from IP space associated with 3xK GmbH.
The probing script used a browser-like Firefox user agent in requests targeting GlobalProtect portals.
The probing script used the user agent Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) in requests targeting Cisco SSL VPN endpoints.
GreyNoise Intelligence analyzes threat activity using its VPN Login Scanner.
